represents the final validated shape of your form data. It defines the full object structure that the form is expected to produce after successful validation and submission.
represents the strongly-typed path (as segment tuples) pointing to a specific leaf inside
without introducing implicit magic, hidden state, or uncontrolled behavior.
The form controller instance returned by
. This is required to access the form's internal state and methods for the specified field.
The field path to the specific field within the form's data structure.
The default value to be used for the field if no initial value is set.
Whether to override any initial value that was set on the form controller, if
was used to set the field's initial value.
the field from the form controller when it is unmounted.
The render function that will be called to render the field UI.

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("name")}
  defaultValue="foo"
  render={({ fieldProps, field, form }) => (
    <input
      {...fieldProps}
      disabled={form.controller.isSubmitting}
      type="text"
      placeholder="John"
      className={field.issues.length !== 0 ? "text-red-500" : "text-green-500"}
    />
  )}
/>;

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("email")}
  render={({ fieldProps }) => (
    <input {...fieldProps} type="email" placeholder="john@example.com" />
  )}
/>;

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("age")}
  render={({ field }) => (
    <input
      type="number"
      value={field.value ?? ""}
      onChange={(e) => {
        const parsed = Number(e.target.value);
        field.setValue(Number.isNaN(parsed) ? undefined : parsed);
      }}
      onFocus={() => field.touch()}
      onBlur={() => field.validate()}
    />
  )}
/>;

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("country")}
  render={({ field }) => (
    <CustomSelect
      selected={field.value}
      options={countryOptions}
      onSelect={(option) => field.setValue(option.code)}
      onClose={() => field.touch()}
    />
  )}
/>;
and gets out of the way.

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("age")}
  render={({ field }) => (
    <input
      type="number"
      value={field.value ?? ""}
      onChange={(e) => {
        const parsed = Number(e.target.value);

        field.setValue(Number.isNaN(parsed) ? undefined : parsed);
        // ^ it'll still trigger validation
      }}
      onBlur={() => field.markAsTouched()}
    />
  )}
/>;
Whether the form has attempted submission
A minimal text input with submission-aware disabling.

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("name")}
  render={({ fieldProps, form }) => (
    <input
      {...fieldProps}
      type="text"
      placeholder="John Doe"
      disabled={form.controller.isSubmitting}
    />
  )}
/>;
Direct value binding for boolean fields.

<FieldRenderer
  form={form}
  path={form.path.of("acceptTerms")}
  defaultValue={false}
  render={({ field }) => (
    <label>
      <input
        type="checkbox"
        checked={!!field.value}
        onChange={(e) => field.setValue(e.target.checked)}
        onFocus={() => field.touch()}
      />
      I agree to the terms
    </label>
  )}
/>;

FieldRenderer — Typed React Field Binding | Goodie Forms
Detailed reference for the FieldRenderer component in Goodie Forms. Learn how to bind strongly-typed fields to React with full control, minimal abstraction, and explicit validation behavior.
